item.title: Potato's Daily Dirties
item.description:
The above image was made by the wonderful CammyJay! They have their own channel in the deviousness discord where they share artwork and sometimes do drawings based on word prompts given by members. its a lot of fun! I has a Deviousness discord server! We do have a hard rule of no politics and/or religion. The discord is a safe space to get away from the bullshit going on. We have a bunch of areas including a SFW area, content creator area, a NSFW area and more! We have gaming fun! We have spontaneous gaming and some ping roles for people to use to get groups together based on US and EU time zones! We have movie nights on Saturdays! So feel free to wander in and say haii! Thank you to everyone who has joined and made it such a unique and special place! Deviousness discord that is 18+: https://discord.gg/deviousness If you have issues with link see dis: https://imgur.com/a/8GUNAz4 Deviousness steam group: https://steamcommunity.com/groups/DeviousnessDiscord
artist of work above (aside from my signing potato on it): https://florahv.artstation.com/projects/XnL8P3 have been traveling a lot. so if anyone has united milageplus things they wont use like miles/pluspoints they wouldnt mind gifting or using for my future travel things lemme know via dm here. I am on dis now and trying to remember to linky over there too for when I post and also post the memes that get removed: https://bsky.app/profile/potatocheesmonk.bsky.social I has a post with links to all my past posts. enjoy: https://imgur.com/a/ZDA2CBe #potato #memes #dailydirties #makinmywaydowntown #holdinhandstothefrontpage
item.dc:creator: PotatoCheesMonk

item.title: You be careful what you wish for.
item.description: Is ded. One love.
item.dc:creator: Bruskey

item.title: Good hearted little one.
item.description: When Jude Baker was 12, life changed overnight. The middle schooler from Summerville, Georgia, was diagnosed with Ewing sarcoma, a rare and aggressive cancer that attacks bones and the tissue around them. What followed was a two-year fight that included surgery to remove a tumor, nearly a year of chemotherapy, and all the physical and emotional weight that comes with treatment at such a young age. After finishing treatment, Jude rang the bell at the hospital to mark the end of chemo. He is now 14 and in remission. Because he faced a life-threatening illness, he became eligible for a wish through the Make-A-Wish Foundation, which grants special experiences to kids ages 3 to 17 with critical conditions. Most kids pick a trip to a theme park, a chance to meet a favorite athlete or musician, or a gift they’ve always wanted. Jude had a different idea. While going to appointments, he had noticed people experiencing homelessness near the medical centers. That stuck with him. He told his family and the Make-A-Wish team that if he got one wish, he wanted to use it to help those individuals. Make-A-Wish Georgia coordinators later said community service wishes are not something they usually offer. Jude didn’t have a backup wish. This was the only thing he asked for. He told organizers, “I got out of my version of heck, and I want to help others who are in their own version."
item.dc:creator: kenistod

item.title: President Nixon declared, "I am not a crook," during a televised Q&A session on Nov 17, 1973, with newspaper execs at Disney World. Becoming a defining, controversial and mocked moment in his presidency. Funny how history repeats itself.
item.description: A wordy title for just a meme by MeidasTouch.
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.dc:creator: Violetcosplay

item.title: I'm just extremely proud of myself
item.description: Starting in November 2025 I started a 5k training app. Zombies run 5k. It took me 4 months to complete and I made a post when I jogged for 10 minutes. In March I signed up for my first 5k in April that gave me about 5 weeks to get ready. So each week I did 3 Weeks of jogging, learning about how to jog just fast enough to keep my heart rate at a certain BPM because most 5k apps seem to forget that part is really important. Each week I did small interval jogging like one week I did a 10 minute warmup walk, then jog starting at about 8 minutes, walk 3 minutes, then jog 6 minutes, walk 3 minutes, 5 minute jog, then 10 minute cool down. This got a bit longer each week. A week before the 5k I did a test on a friday where I had to jog for 1 mile maintaining my heart rate at under 155bpm, mile 2 was trying to keep it under 160, then mile 3 under 165. When I completed it I remember thinking "Holy shit, did I really just do that?" Proving to myself I could do it. The following week I did runs 3 days, walk days. It was an easy week so 5k day I had energy. Saturday I planned out everything for the Sunday 5k. When I woke up, what time I ate, drank. Clothes laid out. I made coldbrew on Friday, overnight oats on Saturday night. I had the course memorized right down to the hills I'd come across, preparing myself to what I'd do like walk up the hill to reserve energy, and run down on the other side. I created a playlist that would start off with slower music, but each song got more and more intense with the idea of keeping up with me on race day so I wouldn't have fast paced music to start and then run too fast. I was really anxious and needed to focus on what i had control of. Hell I even got ocd and used a tape measure to place my bib on my shirt dead center down to the millimeter(I prefer metric for measuring even though I'm American). Race day arrives, and I execute everything that morning. Get up, eat breakfast, drink coffee, pound a gatorade, shower, put on my race clothes, my running belt, put my headphones in my pocket, and then leave at 7am on the dot. My wife and kids were kind enough to come support me as well as a family friend and my wife's cousin which honestly meant a lot. As I get there I'm nervous still and I must pee 4 times before the race including 1 last time 5 minutes before the race. Right before the race, I start my watch to help me keep my heart rate below certain thresholds, turn on my music, and then like that I just focus on nothing else but my jogging after we get out of start line. The first mile felt like it took an hour. There was so many people in my way that I would try and find gaps to slip past them so I could maintain my pace, trying to keep my heart rate below 155 to start so I wouldn't burn out. After mile 1 was done, mile 2 felt so fast with lots of room on the route and not needing to jump past people, still jogging and only slowing down to a walk occasionally to get my heart rate back down below my threshold, then jogging almost as soon as it lowers back to the target rate. Finally I hit mile 3, and a little past half way I made the mistake of jogging much faster up hill than I had planned and spiking my heart rate so I had to walk for about 2 minutes to get it back down. But finally at the end of mile 3, I release the breaks and haul ass for the last 0.1 mile. As I cross the finish line, I look up like "Holy shit, I did it!" There are photos I saw where my face looks shocked, but then I smile. It was a lot of emotions. I grab my medal off the table, my adrenaline starting to come down, as I look at my wife I suddenly start crying tears of joy. I'm sweaty as hell, hugging my wife, and just crying on her shoulder. I was so proud of myself. I worked so hard and seeking out to do something I have never been able to do. I ran the race at 230lbs. I've already signed up for my next race in may so my journey isn't over yet. My goal for the race was a 50 minute 5k. My race time was 50:36 which pissed me off when I found out cause 36 seconds was probably the time from being crowded with a bunch of people. Anyways, sorry this was so long. TLDR: I trained hard for 5 months to run a 5k while losing weight and completed it. My playlist for the race Own Appeal – Oddisee Stick Season – Noah Kahan Fell On Black Days – Soundgarden The Way I Am – Eminem Criminal – Eminem Burden In My Hand – Soundgarden Black Hole Sun – Soundgarden DarkSide – Bring Me The Horizon Obey (feat. YUNGBLUD) – Bring Me The Horizon A Drug Against War – KMFDM Ultra – KMFDM Toxicity – System Of A Down Duality – Slipknot Dig – Mudvayne Psychosocial – Slipknot Happy? – Mudvayne
item.dc:creator: PepperoniAndFingernailPizza

item.title: Fynn Jackson is an origami artist known for creating incredibly detailed paper sculptures, often folding expressive faces and complex forms from a single sheet of paper.
item.dc:creator: bohund

item.title: Progress update time again!!
item.description: For everyone following my progress relearning how to walk after crushing both legs, feet, left hip, right knee, pelvis, and lower spine (I have more details in my other posts) , I made another huge advance in abilities! I've been doing physical therapy 2x a week to help with gait and balance, but this just happened! Also, peep the fact that I'm starting to slim down a bit and actually found blue jean capris that fit me. As always I'm glad for the people here that I have to share my journey with, and the outpouring of love makes me want to keep going. I actually get less negative comments about the weight I gained while bed bound for 2 years. Like... I see the elephant in the room but I'm working to slim it down:) This journey has been hard but I've learned a lot along the way. Patience, empathy, endurance. It's my hope to be a peer support one day and help others out. I love each and every one of you, internet people. Remember to stop today, take a breath, and remind yourself that it does get better. That you are unique, you are loved, and oh so special.
item.dc:creator: Bloodyvalentine549

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1670 (non-spinary)
item.description:
Let's get this bread. Minimum commentary today as I spent the morning birding near Badger Springs (50 species!), and am on my way to my parent's place to help them pack.
Pippin tax! No Pippin message today; I wasn't going to being him on a long birding hike through predator-rich country even in his backpack. ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Dump of nice and happy stuff, be happy !
item.description:
When rainy days are good for some.
Splashing kids with water.
What a beautiful moment shared between the father, grandpa and the little girl.
A school of fish following a duck.
An overload of cuteness...
New born baby holding doctor's gloves.
Long legs vs long torso...
Long Rope Skipping Has Really Improved Over The Years.
Henry Ford driving the first car he built in 1896.
Self driving car fail to stop at stop sign and run over mannequin ....
Zebra crossing ...
Cargo Vespa.
The Peel P50 was the smallest car ever produced in series. From the Isle of Man. Weighted 59 kg and had no reverse, you had to left it by hand !
Roads are unsafe.
The sponsors need him.
Traditional Chinese "puzzle ball" and the special chisel to make them.
This tool to fix a hole in a knitted material.
Splitting firewood.
ISS Speed at Ground Level.
Take out a drone with an anti-drone drone.
A welcome ally has joined the fight against the dystopian AI surveillance state : Birds !!!
Vintage Metal Planer.
Infinity flying paper.
Gummy moulds. To replace your teeth after destroying them ?
This operator is way too skilled for his job.
Victorian washing machine.
These guys are forming a human ball.
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: GovBrief Today #451
item.description: NBC News reported tonight a number the Pentagon has spent weeks refusing to give Congress: $5 billion in damage to American military bases from Iran's strikes. In 2021, the World Food Program told the world's richest man it would take six billion dollars to keep 42 million people from starving. Elon Musk passed. The US government never wrote the check either. Now it's writing one for $5 billion in damage, on top of the $11.3 billion the war's first six days already cost. The bill is going to keep growing, and the Pentagon wants a record budget to pay it. Call your representatives through 5 Calls and Resistbot and demand a full accounting before Congress approves another dollar. I've emailed 7,144 stories over 451 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your name at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Easy click links, data, images at the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260426-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-apr26 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/sc-measles-over [3] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-damage [4] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-ballroom [5] https://go.govbrief.today/us-colombia-gold [6] https://go.govbrief.today/mccormick-reelection Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
